texto: [A] couple I had known - who were old friends - asked me what I was going to work on next. I told them I wanted to write a near future book about AIDS concentration camps. They were vehement in their response: they thought it was a terrible idea. Their words both shocked and saddened me. "Do you really want to write a book about homosexuals?" they asked me. "Won't people who read your work be influenced toward sin?"I notice that I don't hear from them much lately.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Tracy Hickman que provoca limites e escolhas conscientes. Ao questionar “[A] couple I had known - who were old friends - asked me what I was going to work on next. I told…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre amizade, aids, gay-rights, com foco em termino e trabalho.

texto: Raistlin opened his eyes, looking at her without recognition. And in them, she saw deep, undying sorrow--the look of one who has been permitted to enter a realm of deadly, perilous beauty, and who now finds himself, once more, cast down into the grey, rain-swept world.
explicacao: Citação de Tracy Hickman sobre temas ligados a autoestima. “Raistlin opened his eyes, looking at her without recognition. And in them, she saw deep, undying …” articula autoestima, perilous-beauty, sorrow em registro de autoestima, com relevância para contextos como dia-chuvoso e sorrow.
